an7.50:1.1: Then Jāṇussoṇin the brahman went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
an7.50:1.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
an7.50:1.3: “Does Master Gotama claim to be one who leads the holy life?”
an7.50:1.4: “If, brahman, one could rightly say of anyone,
an7.50:1.5: ‘He leads the holy life without gap, without break, without spot, without blemish—perfect & pure,’ it would rightly be said of me.
an7.50:1.6: I lead the holy life without gap, without break, without spot, without blemish—perfect & pure.”
an7.50:1.7: “But what, Master Gotama, is a gap, a break, a spot, a blemish of the holy life?”
an7.50:2.1: “There is the case, brahman, where a certain contemplative or brahman, while claiming to be one who rightly follows the holy life, doesn’t actually engage in copulating with a woman
an7.50:2.2: but he does consent to being anointed, rubbed down, bathed, or massaged by a woman.
an7.50:2.3: He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.
an7.50:2.4: This is a gap, a break, a spot, a blemish of the holy life.
an7.50:2.5: He is called one who lives the holy life in an impure way, one who is fettered by the fetter of sexuality. He is not freed from birth, aging, & death, from sorrows, lamentations, pains, griefs, & despairs. He is not freed, I tell you, from suffering & stress.
an7.50:3.2: he jokes, plays, and amuses himself with a woman. He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.…
an7.50:4.2: he stares into a woman’s eyes. He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.…
an7.50:5.2: he listens to the voices of women outside a wall as they laugh, speak, sing, or cry. He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.…
an7.50:6.2: he recollects how he used to laugh, converse, and play with a woman. He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.…
an7.50:7.2: he sees a householder or householder’s son enjoying himself endowed with the five strings of sensuality. He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.…
an7.50:8.2: he practices the holy life intent on being born in one or another of the deva hosts, (thinking) ‘By this virtue or practice or abstinence or holy life I will be a deva of one sort or another.’
an7.50:8.3: He enjoys that, wants more of that, and luxuriates in that.
an7.50:8.4: This is a gap, a break, a spot, a blemish of the holy life.
an7.50:8.5: He is called one who lives the holy life in an impure way, one who is fettered by the fetter of sexuality. He is not freed from birth, aging, & death, from sorrows, lamentations, pains, griefs, & despairs. He is not freed, I tell you, from suffering & stress.
an7.50:9.1: “And, brahman, as long as I saw that one or another of these seven fetters of sexuality was not abandoned in myself, I did not claim to have directly awakened to the right self-awakening unexcelled in the cosmos with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, their royalty & commonfolk.
an7.50:10.1: But when I did not see any one of these seven fetters of sexuality unabandoned in myself, then I did claim to have directly awakened to the right self-awakening unexcelled in the cosmos with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, their royalty & commonfolk.
an7.50:10.3: ‘Unprovoked is my release. This is the last birth. There is now no further becoming.’”
an7.50:11.1: When this was said, Jāṇussoṇin the brahman said to the Blessed One,
an7.50:11.3: May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
